title: Derivation — how a set was produced from a parent set
description:
First-class record of ONE derivation operation. Links a parent_set (or parents) to a child set via an operation_type + operation_params + optional filter_profile + analysis_purpose. Decouples the operation identity from the child set: a derivation_id can be reused if two sets are produced the same way, and two derivations with the same parent + operation_type + params + filter_profile + coordinate_system must produce the same hash. Per SETS_AND_ANALYSES.md §'Derivation as a first-class record'. One JSON per derivation under <registry_root>/derivations/<derivation_id>.json.

The distinction from set_v1.derived_from (the inline set-algebra form) is intentional: set-algebra ops (intersect / union / difference / filter / from_set / from_inline) compose pure logical combinations and have no software / random-seed / parameter dependencies. Operation-style derivations (thin_by_distance, window_extract, random_sample, callability_filter, …) DO depend on a parameter bundle, possibly a random seed, a software version, and an analysis purpose — those go through derivation_id + operation_params_id.

derivation_id.description: Stable identifier. Convention: 'derive_<what>_<scope>_v<N>' (e.g. 'derive_beagle_thin500_global_v1', 'derive_lostruct_LG28_v1'). Reusable across child sets that share the same recipe.

label.description: Human-readable display label for the recipe.

operation_type.description: Canonical name of the operation kind. Examples: 'thin_by_distance', 'window_extract', 'random_sample', 'callability_filter', 'maf_filter', 'sort_then_take_n', 'concordance_intersect'. Open vocabulary — atlases may add their own. NOT for pure set algebra (intersect / union / difference / filter / from_set / from_inline) — those stay inline on set_v1.derived_from.

parent_set_id.description: FK into set_v1.set_id. The set this derivation reads FROM. For multi-parent operations (rare; usually set-algebra handles those inline), use parent_set_ids[] instead.

parent_set_ids.description: When the operation reads from multiple parent sets (e.g. concordance_intersect over two callsets). When set, parent_set_id should be omitted (or set to the primary parent).

operation_params_id.description: FK into operation_params_v1.operation_params_id. The bundle of parameter values used (distance_bp, sort_key, choice_rule, seed, …). Null when the operation has no parameters.

filter_profile_id.description: Optional FK into a filter-profile registry (e.g. 'popstruct_angsd_sites_v1', 'maf005_callable09_v1'). Captures pre-derivation site/sample filtering. Free-form id string until a formal filter_profile_v1 schema is added; the inventory page treats it as a labelled chip.

coordinate_system.description: Genome assembly id when the operation is coordinate-aware (most variant_site / window / interval / breakpoint / gene operations are). Null for entity types without coordinates (sample / bam / chromosome).

analysis_purpose.description: Why this derivation exists. Free-form short tag (e.g. 'global population structure', 'local PCA inversion scan', 'ngsRelate kinship QC'). Distinguishes two derivations with the same operation+params from one another when they were created for different downstream analyses.

software.description: Software / tool that performed the derivation (e.g. 'plink2', 'bcftools', 'angsd', 'custom-thin.py'). Informational; the actual binary path / version go in software_version + software_repo.

software_repo.description: Optional path or URL to the software source.

produces_set_id.description: Optional FK into set_v1.set_id — the primary child set this derivation produced (filled when the child set is registered). Lets set lookups go both directions.

output_hash.description: sha256 of the canonical produced-set member representation (sorted ids, '\n'-joined). Matches set_v1.hash on the produced set. Two derivations with the same parent_set + operation_type + operation_params + filter_profile + coordinate_system MUST converge to the same output_hash (and therefore should reuse the same derivation_id).

_examples._description: BEAGLE-input thinning to 1 site per 500 bp, deterministic, for global popstructure analyses.
_examples.label: BEAGLE thinning — first valid site per 500 bp, per chromosome
_examples.analysis_purpose: BEAGLE input for ngsRelate / NGSadmix / PCA

_examples._description: Local-PCA window extraction on LG28 for lostruct.
